The best use of the provided atomic propositions is to reference 'powerConnected' as indicating external power status, so the transition of interest is when 'powerConnected' becomes false (i.e., external power loss). The structured property captures that for 12 ticks after 'powerConnected' is false (i.e., '!powerConnected'), the system must keep operating (represented as 'ventilating').

explanation: The requirement states that after loss (failure) of external power (not directly represented in atomic propositions), the system should continue to operate using an internal power source for 12 time units. However, since 'off' indicates system is de-energized, we can require 'not off' for 12 ticks after 'powerFailure'.
